scope.allowedChanges: Documentation files inside the target folder only

keyDetails: soot near vents and upper seams

unityConstruction.source: Unity primitives, ProBuilder-style meshes, procedural mesh helpers, and existing project material systems
primitivePlan: Flattened cubes for iron back plates, rails, anchor blocks, and clamp blocks
Cylinders for steam or hydraulic cylinder bodies and piston rods
Small cylinders or UV spheres for rivets, bolts, and hinge knuckles
Capsules or short cylinders for amber indicator lenses
Decals, transparent planes, vertex colors, or material masks for oil, soot, and grime

blackenedIron.baseColor: near-black charcoal with heat-worn blue-gray or brown variation
blackenedIron.notes: Dominant structural material with chipped edges, pitting, soot, and scraped corners

agedBrass.baseColor: muted brass, dark ochre, aged gold, brown tarnish
agedBrass.notes: Visible piston rods and caps with oxidation in recesses, not clean yellow gold

cylindersAndSeals.notes: Cylinder collars should show oil shine around moving rod seals

amberIndicators.notes: Readable as diegetic pressure status, not a gameplay prompt or objective marker

grimeAndSoot.oil: dark brown-black glossy vertical streaks below seals and pivots
grimeAndSoot.soot: matte charcoal deposits near vents, upper seams, and heat-exposed edges

acceptanceCriteria: Reads immediately as heavy steampunk pressure-door side bracing
Center route remains visually legible and physically unobstructed
Blackened iron dominates the structural silhouette
Aged brass piston rods are clearly visible
Hydraulic or steam cylinder bodies are present on both sides
Rivets and bolts communicate industrial scale
Oil grime and soot appear in plausible mechanical locations
Small amber pressure indicators exist on both sides and remain subtle
No gameplay authority components are included
No trigger colliders, route-lock scripts, nav blockers, damage scripts, or interaction prompts are included
Construction can be built or revised inside Unity using primitives, ProBuilder, or procedural generation
Materials remain readable under warm brassworks lighting and darker combat lighting
validationChecklist: Files for this slice live only under Documentation/AssetProduction/PistonDoorBracePrototype
No existing code, scenes, build scripts, release notes, or shared status docs were edited
Planned hierarchy contains visual geometry and editor-only helpers only
Any future collider proposal is non-authoritative and does not block the route aperture
Mirrored left and right braces avoid obvious repetition in grime and wear
Rivets are instanced or kept within an environment dressing budget
Amber indicators do not resemble quest markers, pickups, buttons, or route locks
Soot and oil placement follows heat, gravity, and mechanical contact points

sceneSetup: Target gameplay camera height and FOV
Dim cool fill from the route interior
Subtle amber practical near one pressure indicator
requiredCaptures: Close-up of piston rod, clevis, rivets, grime, and amber indicator
unityTestRenderEvaluation.passCondition: The test render makes the threshold feel reinforced, oily, soot-stained, and mechanically pressurized while leaving gameplay ownership to separate systems.

handoff.notes: This status file documents the target only; no Unity project assets or gameplay systems were created by this slice.
